text: 0.6g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
77.4g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
12.8g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
magnesium at 11% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

pros first: usually affordable and scalable. practicality matters for staple foods. useful staple energy source. that matters because grains are often judged by how practical they are in real diets. works as a meal anchor. a lot of value comes from being easy to build around.

now the downsides: easy to overshoot portions. staple carbs can pile up fast without much friction. needs strong pairings to feel complete. the grain rarely finishes the nutrition job alone. carb load is still the main burden. that is the category risk no grain fully escapes.
B tier. Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
